_schema_notes.coverage: Authoritative schema for all DID formats — DD Form 1664 (pre-2014) and free-text (post-2014 MIL-STD-963C)
_schema_notes.did_format: Set to 'dd_form_1664' or 'free_text'. Determines which fields are expected to be populated.
_schema_notes.963c_compliant: True if the DID was prepared under MIL-STD-963C (2014 or later). False for older DIDs.
_schema_notes.reserved_fields: Fields marked RESERVED are present in one format but not the other. Always include them — set to empty string or empty array when not applicable.
_schema_notes.preparation_instructions: Official MIL-STD-963C name for what was previously called 'requirements'. Contains the numbered sections of the DID. All section numbers are normalized to strictly numeric dot-notation (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Letter-based source numbering (2.a, 3.1.b) is converted to numeric equivalents (2.1, 3.1.2).
_schema_notes.asterisk: True if the requirement number is followed by * in the source document (conditional requirement).

title: Department OF Defense (Dod) Standard Practice Standard Documents
summary: This Data Item Description specifies format and content instructions for DoD Standard Practice Standards, covering revisions, changes, and various notices for their preparation when required on contract.
use_relationship: A DoD Standard Practice Standard will be used to specify military unique design or functional criteria that must be adhered to in the development of systems, subsystems, equipment, assemblies, components, items, or parts. This Data Item Description (DID) contains the format and content preparation instructions for DoD Standard Practice Standards and their associated documents that are described in the following paragraphs of MIL-STD-962. Only those documents listed below will be required when specified individually on contract. DoD Standard Practice Standard - Paragraphs 4.1 through 5.15. DoD Standard Practice Standard Revision - Paragraph 5.17. DoD Standard Practice Standard Change - Paragraphs 5.18 through 5.18.7. DoD Standard Practice Standard Inactive for New Design Notice - Paragraph 5.19.2. DoD Standard Practice Standard Cancellation Notice - Paragraph 5.19.3. DoD Standard Practice Standard Reinstatement Notice - Paragraph 5.19.4. DoD Standard Practice Standard Reactivation Notice - 5.19.5. DoD Standard Practice Standard Administrative Notice - Paragraph 5.19.6.
distribution_statement: DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A: Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.

preparation_instructions.title: Reference Documents
preparation_instructions.content: The applicable issue of the documents cited herein, including their approval dates and dates of any applicable notices and revisions, shall be as specified in the contract.

preparation_instructions.title: Format and Content for DoD Standard Practice Standards shall be as follows:

children.title: DoD Standard Practice Standard. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standards shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraphs 4.1 through 5.15.
DoD Standard Practice Standard Revision: Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Revisions shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraph 5.17.
DoD Standard Practice Standard Changes. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Changes shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraphs 5.18 through 5.18.7.

DoD Standard Practice Standard Inactive for New Design Notices. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Inactive for New Design Notices shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraph 5.19.2.
DoD Standard Practice Standard Cancellation Notices. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Cancellation Notices shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraph 5.19.3.
DoD Standard Practice Standard Reinstatement Notices. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Reinstatement Notices shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraph 5.19.4.
DoD Standard Practice Standard Reactivation Notices. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Reactivation Notices shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraph 5.19.5.
DoD Standard Practice Standard Administrative Notices. Format and content of DoD Standard Practice Standard Administrative Notices shall be in accordance with MIL-STD-962, paragraph 5.19.6.
